finding_results.notes: The general-library survey now points Hermione to an archive-only catalogue mark, the retrieval happens through the open gated archive, and the folio is reshelved back into archive 14-C before Draco returns.
The chapter now ends on Hermione's conscious withholding in the library, with the lie named internally as concealment rather than routed into another insomnia close.
The scene opens on the new diagnostic pattern and immediate probe instead of re-teaching instrument layout, sleeve-rolling, or table setup.
The repeated 'sharp angles' facial descriptor was removed and replaced with a moment-specific observation about Draco's mouth under strain.

The only retained Mark-pain-adjacent beat is the new information that Hermione's touch has become bodily relief from Draco's side of the channel; the chapter no longer keeps re-establishing the baseline ache.

Literal resonance references were cut to the new bidirectional breach, the archive cross-reference, and the folio's technical explanation; the rest of the chapter now focuses on consequence rather than repeatedly naming the phenomenon.
The prose was recast with periods, commas, and colons. No em-dashes remain in the revised chapter.
Sentence entries were rebuilt around varied subjects and paragraph pivots. The revised chapter reduces She-open sentence starts to 9.

The verbatim style-bible example line "Mm." (snapshot line 13) was already replaced during the prior revision pass. The current line reads: Draco kept his eyes on the book propped beside his hand. "Go on." — which conveys distracted assent, is not "Mm.", and is not near-verbatim to any Draco example line in the style bible. The surrounding context (eyes on book, minimal directive response to Hermione's "I'm going to push lower") preserves his guarded, distracted register. No further revision required.

finding_results.notes: Compressed the post-folio doubt into one statement, replaced repeated mechanism paraphrase with concrete spatial/domestic consequences, added a visible concealment beat at the table, and roughened Hermione's rationalization into an on-page self-correction before the conscious lie lands.

pass_summaries.summary: All packet findings were addressed in the chapter revision: the exam opening now starts on new data, the bonding folio is canonically retrieved from the gated archive, repeated Mark/resonance language was compressed, em-dash and She-open density were reduced below target, and the chapter now closes on Hermione's active concealment rather than a bedroom insomnia tag.

Single finding (CH11_DIALOGUE_EXAMPLE_LINE_COPY) was already resolved by the prior pass: "Mm." replaced with "Go on." at chapter_11.md:9. Full dialogue-register audit confirmed: both characters' contraction levels honored (Hermione high, Draco moderate), no stiff uncontracted forms in casual dialogue, voice separation intact, productive roughness preserved, no style-bible example lines reproduced anywhere in the chapter. No additional dialogue or idiolect defects identified.
Revised chapter 11 to remove the repetitive doubt-restatement loop after the folio reveal while preserving the discovery, concealment, and information-asymmetry setup.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=10, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
